task_completion.description: Open the required climate datasets in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and the datasets 'air.mon.ltm.nc' and 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' are visible in the Sources/Datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create a temperature map for July
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of 'air' (temperature). The time/date control or plot title must indicate the month of July (e.g., '0000-07-01' or time index corresponding to July).

task_completion.description: Create a precipitation map for July
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of 'prate' (precipitation rate). The time/date control or plot title must indicate the month of July.

task_completion.description: Export the temperature and precipitation maps as PNG files
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Panoply exporting files as 'temperature_july.png' and 'precipitation_july.png', or a file manager/terminal showing these two files successfully created in the ~/Documents/MalariaSuitability/ directory.

task_completion.description: Write and save the transmission suitability report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) showing the contents of 'transmission_suitability_report.txt' with analysis mentioning regions like 'Africa' and 'Asia', or a file manager/terminal showing the file exists in ~/Documents/MalariaSuitability/.

integrity.description: Agent used Panoply to generate the maps
integrity.visual_evidence: The Panoply graphical user interface is visible in the screenshots being actively used to plot the .nc files. The agent should not use Python scripts (e.g., matplotlib/xarray) in the terminal to bypass the GUI requirement.

integrity.description: Agent wrote a genuine suitability report
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'transmission_suitability_report.txt' contains actual geographical analysis based on the maps (e.g., identifying overlapping suitable zones) rather than just copy-pasting the task description or briefing text.

integrity.description: Agent saved outputs to the correct directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialogs, file manager, or terminal explicitly show the files being placed in '/home/ga/Documents/MalariaSuitability/' and not the Desktop or default Panoply directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing July climatology. The correct output directory is '/home/ga/Documents/MalariaSuitability/'. The expected output files are 'temperature_july.png', 'precipitation_july.png', and 'transmission_suitability_report.txt'. A correct analysis of the data will identify 'Africa' (specifically Sub-Saharan Africa) and 'Asia' (specifically Southern/Southeastern Asia) as the primary regions where suitable temperature (20-30°C) and precipitation conditions overlap for malaria transmission in July.
